findings.title: 'unsafe-inline' in script-src defeats XSS protection
findings.message: Inline <script> blocks and on*= handlers execute — exactly what CSP is meant to stop.
findings.fix: Remove 'unsafe-inline' and use a per-request nonce (`'nonce-…'`) or hashes, plus `'strict-dynamic'`.

findings.title: 'unsafe-eval' allows eval() / new Function()
findings.message: String-to-code execution stays enabled, widening the XSS surface.
findings.fix: Remove 'unsafe-eval' and refactor any eval()/new Function() usage.

findings.title: `https:` scheme source in script-src
findings.message: Any HTTPS origin can serve scripts — barely narrower than a wildcard.
findings.fix: List specific hosts instead of the bare `https:` scheme.

findings.title: Allowlisted host enables a CSP bypass: ajax.googleapis.com
findings.message: ajax.googleapis.com hosts AngularJS (CSP-bypass gadget), so an attacker can run scripts despite the policy.
findings.fix: Drop the host and switch to a nonce + 'strict-dynamic' (which ignores host allowlists).

findings.title: No base-uri
findings.message: An injected <base> tag can rewrite relative script URLs to an attacker's host — bypassing your allowlist.
findings.fix: Add `base-uri 'none'` (or `'self'`). It does not fall back to default-src.

findings.title: No frame-ancestors
findings.message: Nothing stops other sites from framing yours (clickjacking). frame-ancestors replaces X-Frame-Options.

findings.title: object-src is not 'none'
findings.message: <object>/<embed> can load plugins (Flash, PDF) that execute scripts and bypass other directives.
findings.fix: Add `object-src 'none'` unless you genuinely embed plugins.

findings.title: 'unsafe-inline' in script-src defeats XSS protection
findings.message: Inline <script> blocks and on*= handlers execute — exactly what CSP is meant to stop.
findings.fix: Remove 'unsafe-inline' and use a per-request nonce (`'nonce-…'`) or hashes, plus `'strict-dynamic'`.

findings.title: Allowlisted host enables a CSP bypass: cdn.jsdelivr.net
findings.message: cdn.jsdelivr.net hosts AngularJS / arbitrary npm packages, so an attacker can run scripts despite the policy.
findings.fix: Drop the host and switch to a nonce + 'strict-dynamic' (which ignores host allowlists).

findings.title: No base-uri
findings.message: An injected <base> tag can rewrite relative script URLs to an attacker's host — bypassing your allowlist.
findings.fix: Add `base-uri 'none'` (or `'self'`). It does not fall back to default-src.

findings.title: No frame-ancestors
findings.message: Nothing stops other sites from framing yours (clickjacking). frame-ancestors replaces X-Frame-Options.

findings.title: object-src is not 'none'
findings.message: <object>/<embed> can load plugins (Flash, PDF) that execute scripts and bypass other directives.
findings.fix: Add `object-src 'none'` unless you genuinely embed plugins.
